task_completion.description: Open the contact_blocks.FCStd file in FreeCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeCAD is open with 'contact_blocks' visible in the window title or tree view. Both 'TopBox' and 'BottomBox' should be visible in the model tree.

task_completion.description: Select the edges of the TopBox
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edges of the 'TopBox' are highlighted (usually in green) in the 3D viewport, or the Chamfer task panel lists multiple edges selected for the operation.

task_completion.description: Apply a 2mm chamfer to all 12 edges of TopBox
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Chamfer' feature appears in the tree view under 'TopBox'. The Chamfer parameters panel shows a size of '2.00 mm'. The 3D model of TopBox shows visibly beveled/angled edges.
'BottomBox' remains in the tree view without any new child features (like Chamfer) added to it, and its 3D appearance remains a simple rectangular box with sharp edges.

task_completion.description: Save the updated document as chamfered_blocks.FCStd
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'chamfered_blocks.FCStd' in the '~/Documents/FreeCAD' directory, or the FreeCAD window title/tree view root updates to 'chamfered_blocks'.

task_completion.description: Export the chamfered TopBox to a STEP file
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' dialog shows the filename 'chamfered_topbox.step' being saved, or a file manager/terminal window shows the file 'chamfered_topbox.step' exists in the '~/Documents/FreeCAD' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the FreeCAD GUI to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with FreeCAD's toolbars, menus, tree view, and 3D viewport rather than just executing a Python script in a terminal or the FreeCAD Python console to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The chamfer operation was genuinely applied to the geometry
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows the TopBox with actual chamfered (flattened) edges, confirming the geometric operation was performed and not just faked by renaming an object to 'Chamfer'.
The tree view shows that only the 'TopBox' (or its Chamfer feature) is selected when the Export dialog is opened, ensuring the BottomBox is not included in the STEP file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/contact_blocks.FCStd'. The target shape to modify is 'TopBox', which has exactly 12 edges. The chamfer size must be exactly 2.0mm. The modified document must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/chamfered_blocks.FCStd'. The chamfered TopBox must be exported to '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/chamfered_topbox.step'.
